Our client based on Thorpe Road was having problems with their dog escaping in the rear garden due to an old and rather worn out fence.
We removed the old fence and installed new featheredge fencing along the bottom of the garden.
We then checked for any other areas the escape expert dog might be able to escape from and made good.
Our client is over the moon and the dog has since become a prisoner of the back garden.

World Superbike at Laguna SecaUSA World Superbike.
Raccia Customs took 1st Place Cafe Racer with this incredible retro moded1967 Kawasaki W1. You'll find this bike featured in the Calendar Bike Garage.

Ephraim Mattos, author and former Navy SEAL, joined Bold TV for our Bookstr segment in honor of Veterans Day. He's served abroad both for the United States and as a civilian, helping with relief and rescue efforts in places like Myanmar where he saw the Rohingya crisis firsthand.
We discussed Mattos' new book, "City Of Death: Humanitarian Warriors in the Battle of Mosul." He wrote the book with Scott McEwen, who also wrote New York Times best-seller, "American Sniper," with Chris Kyle.
Mattos said it was difficult coming home after seeing refugee crises, witnessing violent massacres and getting shot.

While creating a new community (or "subreddit") on Reddit is generally very easy, it's good to have a solid idea of why you're creating the community before moving forward. Here are a few questions we recommend answering before you create your new community.
What purpose will this community serve?
Reddit communities range in size from a handful of subscribers to millions, providing users a place to discuss almost any interest under the sun. Whether your new community will exist as a novelty subreddit, serve a small group of locals, or focus on large common interests that may draw thousands of community members, knowing its purpose can help you determine what ground rules to set and how you can help make your community thrive.
Does an active community with the same purpose already exist?
There are a lot of communities on Reddit, so you may find that the one you wanted to create has already been created. While it's fine to start your own community even if the theme is similar to others, if an identical community exists that's already thriving, you may want to consider joining it, being an active participant in the community, and then volunteering or applying to moderate. This can allow your ideas and efforts to have an immediate impact for an established group of subscribers. If you haven't moderated before, this can also be a great way to gain experience collaborating with a team and understanding what it takes to keep a community running.
If you find that a similar community already exists but isn't moderated, however, consider putting in a request to reclaim it via r/redditrequest.
Am I willing and able to actively moderate this community?
It's important to be willing to moderate any community you plan on building. This helps ensure its good standing if you run into any issues with spam or other site-wide rule breaking content in your community. If you have a great idea for a new community but are short on resources or unfamiliar with moderation, you'll want to plan for adding moderators in the near future to help your community flourish. Keep in mind that our moderator guidelines require stable and active mod teams; without that, your community could be reclaimed in r/redditrequest.
Ready to get started? Read on to find out how to create your subreddit.

Photo courtesy of Brett Lowell/Big Up Productions.
"Hey guys, Chris Sharma did his arch project in Mallorca today. He hasn't named it yet  -  he's still in the bar celebrating ... " read the email that filmmaker Josh Lowell wrote Climbing on September 26. Sharma, who described his "arch project," a 65-foot deep-water solo, in the latest issue of Climbing (No. 252), had worked on the climb during four different trips to the Spanish isle.
"Since I started deep-water soloing," Sharma said, "I've wanted to find something that's at my limit  -  that's really a project ... I wanted to find something comparable to Realization, taking it to the highest level possible." The arch (Es Pontas, off the east coast of the island) provided such difficulties  -  and then some.
The wildly overhanging line includes a difficult, seven-foot dyno, 35 feet above the sea. (This alone took Sharma roughly 50 attempts.) He had linked up through the dyno and out an easier, 5.13a face finish, but didn't feel that this was the integral route, which climbs up through the dyno and continues out the belly of the arch via technical heel hooking and powerful slapping. Sharma said that the climbing remains difficult until the 55-foot mark.
In typical Sharma style, he declined to grade the arch, but his own comparisons to the world's first legitimate 5.15a give some perspective to its difficulty.

Dopamine 2.0 Preview 5 is available!
- [Changed] Removed song covers in playlists. Loading so many images in memory causes a huge memory footprint.
- [Fixed] Fixed an issue deleting entries from playlists, after files have been removed from or renamed on disk.

I'm pretty happy about dopamine. I honestly think it's the best music player i've stumbled upon. I wanted to thank you for that first.
Now, you see... on page 98 of this thread, you mentioned that you fixed a bug related to MiniLyrics, and I wanted to ask for your help, because I can't make MiniLyrics detect your player.
Those two are compatible? If that's the case, can you please teach me or show me how to make it work? Thanks.
Dopamine doesn't work together with Minilyrics. Dopamine however has a built-in lyrics downloader and displays the lyrics in the now playing screen.
